tasks.description: Add multi-generation shot creation path to `src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/`. (1) Add `CreateShotWithGenerationsPathInput` type to `shotCreationTypes.ts` with fields: `selectedProjectId`, `shotName`, `generationIds: string[]` (ordered), `shots`, `queryClient`, `createShot` (the empty-shot mutation). (2) Add `createShotWithGenerationsPath()` to `shotCreationPaths.ts`: create empty shot via `createShot()`, then SEQUENTIALLY call `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration(shotId, genId)` for each generation in order (sequential is critical — parallel would race on auto-position calculation). Return `ShotCreationResult` with all generation IDs. (3) Extend `CreateShotOptions` in `shotCreationTypes.ts` with `generationIds?: string[]`. (4) Wire into `shotCreationAction.ts` (`useCreateShotAction`): when `generationIds?.length` is set and no files, dispatch to the new path. Existing `generationId` (singular) path must remain unchanged. Result: `useShotCreation().createShot({ generationIds: [...] })` creates a shot with all items auto-positioned in array order using existing `generateShotName()` convention.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `generationIds?: string[]` to `CreateShotOptions`, introduced `CreateShotWithGenerationsPathInput`, exported `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration`, and added `createShotWithGenerationsPath()` that creates the shot first and then inserts each generation with an awaited `for...of` loop before invalidating shot-related caches. `useCreateShotAction` now routes ordered multi-generation requests to that path while still deriving the shot name through the existing `generateShotName()` call site in `useShotCreation`. Verified with `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shotCreation src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Verify `VideoGenerationModal` can render outside the travel tool component tree. Inspect `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGenerationModal.tsx` and `useVideoGenerationModalController.ts` for context dependencies beyond the `shot` prop. The modal needs `ProjectContext` (available app-wide) — confirm no travel-tool-specific providers are required. If self-contained, add a brief code comment at the render site in T4/T5. If it depends on travel-tool-only providers, wrap the render site in the minimal required providers.
tasks.executor_notes: Inspected `VideoGenerationModal.tsx`, `useVideoGenerationModalController.ts`, `useShotNavigation.ts`, `useShotSettings.ts`, `useShotImages.ts`, and `AppProviders.tsx`. The modal/controller use QueryClient, ProjectContext, CurrentShotContext, router navigation, shared shot/settings queries, and shared invalidation hooks, all of which are provided app-wide. No travel-tool-only provider dependency was found, so no wrapper changes were required in this batch.

tasks.description: Create `SelectionContextMenu` component at `src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.tsx`. Follow the portal pattern from `ClipAction.tsx`: `createPortal` to `document.body`, fixed position at mouse coords, click-away and Escape dismissal. Props: `position: { x: number; y: number } | null` (null = hidden), `onClose`, `onCreateShot`, `onGenerateVideo`, `isCreating?: boolean`. Two menu items: 'Create Shot' and 'Generate Video' with lucide icons. Style with design tokens: `bg-popover text-popover-foreground border rounded-md shadow-md`. This component is shared by gallery (T4) and timeline (T5). Keep under 150 lines.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `SelectionContextMenu` as a shared `createPortal(..., document.body)` menu with fixed-position rendering, click-away dismissal, Escape dismissal, and token-based popover styling. The component exposes `Create Shot` and `Generate Video` actions with lucide icons and disables actions while `isCreating` is true. Verified with a dedicated RTL test plus the shared shotCreation suite via `npx vitest run src/shared/hooks/shotCreation src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useShotCreation.test.ts src/shared/components/SelectionContextMenu.test.tsx`.

tasks.description: Wire context menu into gallery. (1) Add `onContextMenu?: (e: React.MouseEvent, image: GeneratedImageWithMetadata) => void` to `ItemActions` in `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/types.ts`. (2) In `MediaGalleryItem.tsx`, wire `onContextMenu` on the item's root div, calling `itemActions.onContextMenu?.(e, image)`. (3) Pass through `MediaGalleryGrid.tsx` → `MediaGallery.tsx` → `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx`. (4) In `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx`, implement: `handleContextMenu(e, image)` — preventDefault, check if item is in `selectedGalleryIds` (if not, select it), set `contextMenuPosition` state. On 'Create Shot': extract ordered generation IDs from `gallerySelectionMap` via `Array.from(gallerySelectionMap.values()).map(e => e.generationId)`, call `createShot({ generationIds })`. Clear selection on success. On 'Generate Video': same creation, then set `videoModalShot` state and open `VideoGenerationModal`. (5) Render `SelectionContextMenu` and `VideoGenerationModal` in the component. The modal receives the newly created Shot object.
tasks.executor_notes: Threaded `onContextMenu` through the gallery item prop chain into `GenerationsPaneGallery`, then added a selection-driven context menu flow there. Right-click now single-selects an unselected gallery item first, uses `Array.from(gallerySelectionMap.values()).map(entry => entry.generationId)` to preserve selection order, calls `createShot({ generationIds })`, clears gallery selection only after a successful shot creation, and opens `VideoGenerationModal` with the newly created `Shot` for Generate Video. Verified with `npx vitest run src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Add Create Shot / Generate Video to timeline context menu in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`. (1) Add 'Create Shot' and 'Generate Video' menu items to the existing portal-based context menu. (2) Gate visibility: only show when ALL selected clips have generation assets. Check via `AssetRegistry` — for each selected clip ID, resolve its asset key from `ClipMeta`, look up `registry.assets[assetKey]`, check `entry.generationId` exists. If any selected clip lacks a generationId (text, effect, audio), hide both items entirely. (3) Sort by timeline position before insertion: resolve each selected clip ID to its `TimelineAction` by searching `rows`, sort by `(trackIndex, action.start)`, extract sorted generationIds. (4) Wire to `useShotCreation().createShot({ generationIds: sortedGenIds })`. (5) Render `VideoGenerationModal` — manage modal state (videoModalShot, isModalOpen) same pattern as T4. Access to `useShotCreation` may require lifting it to the parent `TimelineEditor` or using it directly in `ClipAction` — check which component has `ShotsContext` available.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended the existing `ClipAction` portal menu with Create Shot / Generate Video entries while leaving the delete, split, mute, stale-variant, and dismiss actions intact. Timeline shot creation now resolves selected clips against `data.registry.assets`, hides the new actions whenever any selected clip lacks a `generationId`, sorts selected clips by `(trackIndex, action.start)` before extracting generation IDs, and opens the shared `VideoGenerationModal` from `TimelineEditor` with the created `Shot`. Verified with `npx vitest run src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`.

tasks.description: Run tests and validate all changes. (1) Run `npx vitest run` for the full test suite — fix any failures from new props (e.g. `onContextMenu` added to ItemActions may need updating in existing test mocks). (2) Specifically check: gallery tests in `src/features/gallery/`, shot creation tests, MediaGalleryItem behavior tests at `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx`, timeline editor tests. (3) Write a throwaway smoke-test script that imports `createShotWithGenerationsPath` and verifies sequential calling with mock data, run it, then delete it. (4) Verify no regressions to existing gallery selection, drag-and-drop, or timeline context menu actions by reviewing the diff for accidental removals or changes to existing behavior.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the full Vitest suite with `npx vitest run`; it completed in 226.13s with 807 passing files and 26 failing suites / 53 failing tests, all outside the gallery/timeline shot-context-menu feature. Verified the touched areas separately with `npx vitest run src/features/gallery src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationAction.test.ts src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationPaths.test.ts src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCacheUpdate.test.ts src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationEffects.test.ts src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor`, which passed cleanly (16 files / 63 tests). Added a throwaway smoke test that imported `createShotWithGenerationsPath`, mocked insertions, proved the second and third inserts did not start until the prior promise resolved, ran it successfully, then deleted the file. Reviewed the gallery/timeline diff and confirmed the context-menu changes are additive: existing gallery selection behavior, drag-and-drop hooks, and timeline menu actions were not removed or regressed.

watch_items: Sequential insertion order: `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` MUST be called sequentially (not Promise.all) — the RPC auto-calculates position from existing entries, so parallel calls race and produce unpredictable ordering

Positioned items required: VideoGenerationModal filters to positioned images only and blocks with 'At least 1 positioned image is required' if none are positioned. Using `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` with `p_with_position: true` ensures items get timeline_frame values
Shot naming: MUST use existing `generateShotName()` from `useShotCreation` which returns `Shot ${count + 1}` — do NOT invent a new naming convention
Gallery selection order: `gallerySelectionMap` is a Map preserving insertion order — items positioned in click/lasso order. This is correct per plan
Timeline clip sorting: selected clip IDs come from a Set. Before creating shot, resolve each to TimelineAction and sort by (trackIndex, action.start) — do NOT use Set insertion order
Mutual exclusion: gallery and timeline have mutually exclusive selection (selecting in one clears the other). The context menu reads from whichever system currently has a selection, never both
Non-media clip gating: context menu items MUST be hidden entirely when any selected timeline clip lacks a generation asset (text overlays, effects, audio clips)
Right-click unselected item: if user right-clicks an item NOT in the current selection, it should be single-selected first, then context menu shown
DEBT: Do not worsen any existing debt items — particularly watch for: registry-patch-testing, selection-save-bridge, conflict-resolution patterns

VideoGenerationModal provider dependencies: T2 must verify the modal doesn't need travel-tool-specific context. It uses ProjectContext (app-wide) and various hooks — confirm all are available at the gallery render site
Existing ClipAction menu items (delete, split, mute) must not be disturbed — new items are additive only
Context menu dismissal: must dismiss on click-away, Escape, AND after completing an action (all three)

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `createShotWithGenerationsPath()` awaits `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration()` inside a `for...of` loop, and the new branch still uses the existing `generateShotName()` path because `useCreateShotAction` computes `shotName` from `name || generateShotName()` before dispatch.

sense_checks.question: Has the executor confirmed that `VideoGenerationModal` and `useVideoGenerationModalController` don't depend on any provider that's only available in the travel tool tree (e.g. ShotEditorContext, CurrentShotContext)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the modal/controller do not depend on travel-tool-only providers such as ShotEditorContext. The controller only reaches shared app providers/hooks: ProjectContext, QueryClient, CurrentShotContext via `useShotNavigation`, router navigation, shared shot/settings hooks, and invalidation.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the gallery handler single-selects an unselected right-click target first, derives ordered `generationIds` from `gallerySelectionMap` insertion order, clears gallery selection after successful shot creation, and opens `VideoGenerationModal` with the created `Shot` in the Generate Video path.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the timeline menu hides Create Shot / Generate Video when any selected clip resolves to no `generationId`, sorts selected clips by `(trackIndex, action.start)` before creating the shot, and leaves the existing delete, split, mute, and stale-variant actions unchanged.

sense_checks.question: Do all existing tests pass (especially MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx, GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx, and any shot creation tests)? Were test mocks updated to include the new `onContextMenu` prop where needed?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the relevant tests pass: `src/features/gallery` test coverage, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationAction.test.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/shotCreation/shotCreationPaths.test.ts`, related shotCreation tests, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx` / `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`. The touched mocks already accommodate the new `onContextMenu` path, and no additional mock fixes were needed during this verification pass.

meta_commentary:
Key execution guidance:

**T1 is the critical foundation** — get the sequential insertion right. The temptation to use `Promise.all` for speed is strong but will produce random ordering because each `add_generation_to_shot` RPC auto-calculates position from existing entries. A simple for-of loop with await is correct.

**T2 is a quick verification** that unblocks T4 and T5. The exploration suggests VideoGenerationModal only needs ProjectContext (app-wide) and a Shot prop, but verify by tracing `useVideoGenerationModalController` imports. If it needs `useShotNavigation` or `useShotSettings` from ShotsContext, those are also available app-wide via ShotsProvider in AppProviders.

**T3 is straightforward UI** — mirror ClipAction.tsx's portal pattern exactly. Don't use shadcn ContextMenu primitive — the plan specifies the manual portal approach for consistency with the existing timeline menu.

**T4 and T5 are independent and can be developed in parallel** (they share T1-T3 as dependencies but don't depend on each other). The main complexity in T4 is threading `onContextMenu` through 4 component layers (GenerationsPaneGallery → MediaGallery → MediaGalleryGrid → MediaGalleryItem). In T5, the complexity is resolving clip IDs to TimelineActions for sorting — search `rows[i].actions.find(a => a.id === clipId)` to get both the action and trackIndex.

**ShotsContext availability for T5**: `useShotCreation` needs ShotsContext. Check if TimelineEditor or its parents already provide this. If not, the hook may need to be called higher up and passed down as a callback prop.

**The two open plan questions**: (1) Two-step approach (create empty shot + add images) is fine — atomicity isn't critical here since failure mid-way leaves a partially-populated shot which is still valid. (2) For loading feedback, close the context menu immediately and show `isCreating` state — the shot list will update via React Query invalidation when done.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Add multi-generation creation path to shotCreation hooks
Step 2: Verify VideoGenerationModal works outside travel tool tree
Step 4: Wire context menu into gallery (onContextMenu prop threading + handler logic)
Step 5: Handle gallery selection → generation ID resolution
Step 6: Add Create Shot / Generate Video to timeline context menu
Step 7: Test and verify all flows

validation.completeness_notes: Step 5 (gallery selection → generation ID resolution) is folded into T4 as it's inline logic in the context menu handler, not a separate task. All 7 plan steps are covered.
